quiz.title: Unit 6 Quiz 10, Grade 11 Chemistry
quiz.subtitle: Redox Reactions and Balancing
quiz.subject: Chemistry
quiz.topic: Redox Reactions, Oxidation Numbers, Oxidizing and Reducing Agents, Balancing Redox Equations, Reaction Types

questions.question: In the reaction $\text{Cu} + 2\text{AgNO}_3 \rightarrow \text{Cu(NO}_3)_2 + 2\text{Ag}$, which is oxidized?
questions.explanation: Copper is oxidized because its oxidation number increases from 0 in Cu to +2 in $\text{Cu(NO}_3)_2$. Oxidation is the loss of electrons.

questions.question: In the reaction $\text{Cu} + 2\text{AgNO}_3 \rightarrow \text{Cu(NO}_3)_2 + 2\text{Ag}$, which is reduced?
questions.explanation: Silver ions are reduced because their oxidation number decreases from +1 in $\text{AgNO}_3$ to 0 in Ag. Reduction is the gain of electrons.

questions.question: In $2\text{Fe} + 3\text{Cl}_2 \rightarrow 2\text{FeCl}_3$, the Fe changes oxidation number from:
questions.explanation: Iron's oxidation number changes from 0 (elemental form) to +3 in $\text{FeCl}_3$.

questions.question: In $2\text{Fe} + 3\text{Cl}_2 \rightarrow 2\text{FeCl}_3$, the Cl changes oxidation number from:
questions.explanation: Chlorine's oxidation number changes from 0 in $\text{Cl}_2$ to -1 in $\text{FeCl}_3$.

questions.question: Which of the following is a single displacement redox reaction?
questions.explanation: $\text{Zn} + \text{CuSO}_4 \rightarrow \text{ZnSO}_4 + \text{Cu}$ is a single displacement redox reaction. Zinc displaces copper, with Zn oxidized (0 → +2) and Cu reduced (+2 → 0).

questions.question: In $2\text{MnO}_4^- + 5\text{SO}_2 + 2\text{H}_2\text{O} \rightarrow 2\text{MnO}_2 + 2\text{H}_2\text{SO}_4 + 3\text{O}_2$, Mn is:
questions.explanation: Manganese is reduced. In $\text{MnO}_4^-$, Mn has an oxidation number of +7. In $\text{MnO}_2$, Mn has an oxidation number of +4. The oxidation number decreases, so Mn is reduced.

questions.question: In $2\text{MnO}_4^- + 5\text{SO}_2 + 2\text{H}_2\text{O} \rightarrow 2\text{MnO}_2 + 2\text{H}_2\text{SO}_4 + 3\text{O}_2$, S in $\text{SO}_2$ is:
questions.explanation: Sulfur in $\text{SO}_2$ is oxidized. In $\text{SO}_2$, S has an oxidation number of +4. In $\text{H}_2\text{SO}_4$, S has an oxidation number of +6. The oxidation number increases, so S is oxidized.

questions.question: In $2\text{Na} + 2\text{H}_2\text{O} \rightarrow 2\text{NaOH} + \text{H}_2$, the oxidation number of Na changes from:
questions.explanation: Sodium's oxidation number changes from 0 (elemental form) to +1 in NaOH. This is oxidation.

questions.question: In $2\text{Na} + 2\text{H}_2\text{O} \rightarrow 2\text{NaOH} + \text{H}_2$, hydrogen in water is:
questions.explanation: Hydrogen in water is reduced. In $\text{H}_2\text{O}$, H has an oxidation number of +1. In $\text{H}_2$, H has an oxidation number of 0. The oxidation number decreases, so hydrogen is reduced.

questions.question:
___ $\text{H}_3\text{PO}_4$ + ___ $\text{Ca(OH)}_2$ → ___ $\text{Ca}_3(\text{PO}_4)_2$ + ___ $\text{H}_2\text{O}$

What are the correct coefficients for the balanced equation?
questions.explanation: $2\text{H}_3\text{PO}_4 + 3\text{Ca(OH)}_2 \rightarrow \text{Ca}_3(\text{PO}_4)_2 + 6\text{H}_2\text{O}$ is balanced. This is an acid-base neutralization (not redox).

questions.question:
___ $\text{Cu}$ + ___ $\text{HNO}_3$ → ___ $\text{Cu(NO}_3)_2$ + ___ $\text{NO}$ + ___ $\text{H}_2\text{O}$

What are the correct coefficients for the balanced equation?
questions.explanation: $3\text{Cu} + 8\text{HNO}_3 \rightarrow 3\text{Cu(NO}_3)_2 + 2\text{NO} + 4\text{H}_2\text{O}$ is balanced. This is a redox reaction where Cu is oxidized (0 → +2) and N is reduced (+5 → +2).

questions.question:
$\text{CS}_2 + 3\text{O}_2 \rightarrow \text{CO}_2 + 2\text{SO}_2$

This reaction is __________
questions.explanation: This is a combustion reaction (carbon disulfide burning in oxygen). Combustion reactions are always exothermic, releasing heat and often light. The formation of stable products like CO₂ and SO₂ from elements releases energy. Therefore, this reaction is exothermic.

questions.question:
___ $\text{KMnO}_4$ + ___ $\text{HCl}$ → ___ $\text{KCl}$ + ___ $\text{MnCl}_2$ + ___ $\text{Cl}_2$ + ___ $\text{H}_2\text{O}$

What are the correct coefficients for the balanced equation?
questions.explanation: $2\text{KMnO}_4 + 16\text{HCl} \rightarrow 2\text{KCl} + 2\text{MnCl}_2 + 5\text{Cl}_2 + 8\text{H}_2\text{O}$ is balanced. Mn is reduced (+7 → +2) and Cl is oxidized (-1 → 0).

questions.question:
$\text{Zn} + 2\text{AgNO}_3 \rightarrow \text{Zn(NO}_3)_2 + 2\text{Ag}$

What type of reaction is this?
questions.explanation: This is a redox reaction (specifically a single displacement reaction). Zn is oxidized (0 → +2) and Ag is reduced (+1 → 0).

questions.question:
$\text{MgCl}_2 + 2\text{AgNO}_3 \rightarrow 2\text{AgCl} + \text{Mg(NO}_3)_2$

What type of reaction is this?
questions.explanation: This is a double displacement (metathesis) reaction. There is no change in oxidation numbers (Mg remains +2, Cl remains -1, Ag remains +1, NO₃ remains -1), so it is not a redox reaction.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You've mastered redox reactions and balancing complex equations! ⚡🧪
scoreMessages.70: Good job! Review oxidation number changes in redox reactions.
scoreMessages.50: Satisfactory. Focus on identifying oxidized and reduced species.
scoreMessages.0: Please review oxidation numbers, redox reactions, and how to balance redox equations.
